mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.07% as of Jan 2014) reflects an accommodative monetary stance that keeps the equity risk premium compressed via low discount rates, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as long as the rate stays below 0.25%.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / ongoing QE taper
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (65.5% positive) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises near-term consumer spending expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors reprice growth prospects.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.6% at 2014-01-01, down from 7.0% three months prior
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate falls below the unconditional 65.5% base rate over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in INDPRO and PAYEMS growth signals a broad-based expansion in production and employment that jointly confirms durable economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone reliably signals this because industrial output can rise on inventory restocking without labor confirmation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND PAYEMS 3-month average gain > 150k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.03 and PAYEMS=137499k as of Jan 2014 vintage
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized production-employment growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint triggers do not exceed the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST housing starts (999-1002k units, near post-crisis highs) signal strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, but only during early-to-mid expansion regimes; in late-cycle regimes with tightening financial conditions the same signal loses predictive power or reverses as rate-sensitive housing weakens first.
failure_condition: The positive HOUST-return relationship fails to hold (correlation flips sign or becomes insignificant) during identified expansion-regime windows, or 20-day forward returns underperform base rate despite rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 2% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero raises market expectations of accelerated Fed tightening, triggering short-term equity de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation crosses above 2.0% while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation acceleration risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns after CPI crosses the 2% threshold are not significantly negative, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 40% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as corporate earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=411561 million dollars as of Jan 2014 vintage
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods do not outperform the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS shows sustained strong job gains (>200k/month average) concurrent with UNRATE falling toward the Fed's implicit full-employment threshold, markets anticipate an earlier end to zero-rate policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as discount rate expectations rise; this effect requires both series moving together since payroll gains alone do not force a policy response.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.6% approaching 6.5% Evans-rule threshold as of Jan 2014
regime_hint: approach to Fed policy normalization threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint triggers are not significantly negative, or hit rate for negative returns falls below 40% across walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: During the ongoing QE taper regime, a deceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth signals weakening production momentum at a time when monetary support is being withdrawn, amplifying downside risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week; outside of taper/tightening regimes the same deceleration has muted effect because monetary offset is available.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=101.0251 vs revised 100.1599 showing production data volatility during 2014 taper period
regime_hint: QE taper / monetary policy withdrawal regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration during taper regime are not significantly negative relative to base rate, or the effect disappears when restricted to taper-period subsample (p > 0.05).
